humanSummary.text: Re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers after pointer provenance repair. KAIFUU-083 satisfies completion criteria; one P3 diagnostic naming polish item is recorded for future cleanup.

findings.title: Relocation provenance mismatch diagnostic naming is pointer-specific
findings.description: The typed diagnostic code kaifuu.string_relocation.pointer_provenance_mismatch is also used for index-table reference provenance mismatches.

evidence.detail: Re-audit confirmed index-table wrong-target fixtures fail closed, but share the pointer_provenance_mismatch diagnostic name.

findings.impact: The diagnostic is safe and typed, but future users may read an index-table mismatch as pointer-table specific.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: String relocation provenance mismatch diagnostics use a reference-neutral code or document pointer/index applicability.
Pointer-table and index-table wrong-target fixtures continue to fail before output materialization.
Diagnostic naming remains stable or includes a migration note if the code changes.

proposedDagNode.title: Reference-neutral relocation provenance diagnostic
proposedDagNode.summary: Clarify KAIFUU-083 relocation provenance mismatch diagnostics so pointer-table and index-table failures are described without misleading pointer-only wording.
deliverables: Reference-neutral relocation diagnostic code or documented compatibility alias
Pointer-table and index-table diagnostic regression fixtures
acceptanceCriteria: Index-table provenance mismatches do not surface pointer-only wording unless documented as a compatibility alias.
Pointer-table and index-table wrong-target fixtures keep failing before output bytes or output hashes are emitted.
Diagnostic docs or tests state the stable semantic code expected by downstream consumers.
